user_message: Help me build consumption trend for Coho Winery. We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-08 08:40 EST
Subject: Re: consumption trend Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
To: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 09:32 EST
Subject: Re: consumption trend Discussion - Next Steps

Just checking in on the consumption trend proposal. Our VP Engineering is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Eric Kim <eric.kim@cohowinery.com>
To: Ahmed Hassan <ahmed.hassan@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-06 08:41 EST
Subject: consumption trend - Action Required

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about consumption trend. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: consumption trend Discussion - Coho Winery
Date: 2024-01-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Eric Kim (Coho Winery), Ahmed Hassan (Microsoft), Michelle (Coho Winery)

[00:01:15] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Appreciate everyone making time. The main agenda today is consumption trend.

[00:06:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is vendor dependencies.

[00:11:00] Michelle (Coho Winery): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:13:00] Michelle (Coho Winery): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about cost optimization. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:22:00] Eric Kim (Coho Winery): Let me add some context -- we've been working on budget approval and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:24:00] Michelle (Coho Winery): On the consumption trend front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around security compliance.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct consumption trend decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
